title: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
description: Manage infra declaratively with Terraform modules, state, and pipelines.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Terraform itself is infrastructure orchestration software with no direct runtime costs. However, related costs include: (1) Terraform Cloud/Enterprise for remote state management and collaboration: $0 (free tier for small teams up to 5 users) to $70/user/month for team features, typically $200-500/month for a small team; (2) State storage backends like AWS S3 ($0.023/GB + requests, typically $1-5/month); (3) CI/CD pipeline compute for terraform plan/apply runs, typically $10-50/month in GitHub Actions/GitLab CI minutes. Minimum assumes self-hosted state with local backend or free Terraform Cloud tier. Maximum represents Terraform Cloud Team tier for 5-7 users plus CI/CD costs. Does NOT include the actual infrastructure costs that Terraform provisions (compute, databases, etc.), only the Terraform tooling overhead.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity adoption. Factors include: (1) Learning curve: 2-4 weeks for team to learn HCL syntax, state management, module design patterns, and provider APIs; (2) Initial setup: 1-2 weeks to establish remote state backend, CI/CD pipelines, module structure, and workspace organization; (3) Migration effort: 2-4 weeks to translate existing infrastructure to Terraform code if migrating from manual/CloudFormation/other IaC; (4) Best practices: establishing conventions for variable management, secrets handling, module versioning, and state locking; (5) Team training: typically 3-5 engineers need upskilling. Assumes team has some cloud experience but is new to Terraform. Estimate represents ~3-6 weeks of engineering time across team members at blended rate of $150-200/hour.
provenance.licenseCost: Terraform Open Source is free under Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL 2.0). Terraform Cloud offers a generous free tier for up to 5 users with remote state management and basic features. Optional paid offerings: Terraform Cloud Team ($20/user/month), Terraform Cloud Business ($70+/user/month), and Terraform Enterprise (self-hosted, $100k+/year) provide advanced features like policy as code (Sentinel), SSO, and audit logs, but core IaC functionality is fully free. Most small-to-medium teams operate successfully on open source or free tier.

provides.reasoning: Terraform's primary purpose is to provision and manage infrastructure resources declaratively across cloud providers, including compute, networking, storage, and other services.
Terraform manages infrastructure state through state files, tracking the current state of resources and determining what changes need to be applied during plan and apply operations.
Terraform allows defining infrastructure configuration as code through declarative HCL syntax and reusable modules, enabling standardized and repeatable infrastructure definitions.
Terraform's declarative approach and resource replacement patterns enable immutable infrastructure practices where infrastructure is replaced rather than modified in place.
Terraform Cloud/Enterprise with Sentinel provides policy-as-code enforcement for infrastructure compliance, tagging standards, and cost controls.

requires.reasoning: Terraform requires a state backend (local, S3, Terraform Cloud, etc.) to store the infrastructure state file, which is essential for tracking managed resources and enabling team collaboration.
Terraform needs access to cloud provider APIs (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.) to actually provision and manage infrastructure resources through provider plugins.
Version control is essential for IaC best practices, enabling teams to track changes to Terraform configurations, collaborate effectively, and maintain infrastructure history.
The pattern description mentions pipelines as a core component, requiring CI/CD capabilities to automate Terraform plan, apply, and validation workflows.

supports_nfr.reason: Terraform is the recommended pattern for GDPR-regulated multi-cloud environments as it enables reproducible, auditable, code-reviewed infrastructure changes — directly supporting GDPR compliance posture and data residency controls.

Terraform is the recommended pattern for CCPA-regulated environments as it enables reproducible, auditable, code-reviewed infrastructure changes — supporting CCPA compliance posture and data handling controls.

Terraform is the recommended pattern for HIPAA-regulated environments as it enables reproducible, auditable, code-reviewed infrastructure changes with consistent security configurations for PHI environments across cloud providers.

Terraform is the recommended pattern for SOX-regulated environments as it enables reproducible, auditable, code-reviewed infrastructure changes — supporting SOX IT general controls and change management requirements.

supports_constraints.reason: Terraform supports all major cloud providers and on-premises infrastructure through provider plugins.
Terraform is an IaC tool that provisions infrastructure for applications written in any programming language.
Terraform provisions infrastructure that can support any platform type from web apps to IoT to ML workloads.

state_backend.description: Where Terraform state is stored. S3/GCS/Azure for team collaboration with locking, Terraform Cloud for managed state with UI, local for single-user development only.

workspace_strategy.description: How to organize Terraform workspaces. Single for simple setups, per-environment (dev/staging/prod) for isolation, per-service for microservices with independent lifecycles.

module_structure.description: How to organize Terraform modules. Monorepo for simplicity and atomic changes, separate repos for independent versioning, registry for shared/public modules.

ci_cd_integration.description: How Terraform runs are executed. CI/CD platforms for automation, Atlantis for PR-based workflows, Terraform Cloud for managed runs, manual for local execution.

drift_detection.description: Enable scheduled drift detection to identify manual infrastructure changes outside Terraform. Adds operational overhead but improves compliance and state accuracy.
